title: Cyclopeltis cumingiana
taxonomy: Cyclopeltis cumingiana is a species of vine in the family Arum, commonly known as the Philippine vining arum. It is a tropical climbing plant native to the Philippines. The classification of Cyclopeltis cumingiana is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Angiospermophyta, the class Liliopsida, the order Alismatales, the family Arum, the genus Cyclopeltis, and the species C. cumingiana. This plant is also known as Cyclopeltis philippinensis in some sources. Further classification may vary depending on the source used.
uses: Cyclopeltis cumingiana is a species of vine in the family Lomariopsidaceae, and it has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant's roots and leaves are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits and to confirm its safety for human use. Further studies are required to determine the efficacy and optimal dosage of Cyclopeltis cumingiana for medicinal purposes.
